zh.Montgomeryshire, Wales, United Kingdom: 英國威爾斯蒙格莫利夏爾
zh.Montgomeryshire, Wales: 英國威爾斯蒙格莫利夏爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Prince Gruffydd de la Pole - Prince of Upper Powys於1211年出生在英國威爾斯蒙格莫利夏爾時，他的父親Gwenwynwyn Cyfeiliog ap Gwenwynwyn29歲，母親Mary Corbet24歲。他和Mrs. Gruffydd la Pole至少有1個兒子。他去世於1286年在英國威爾斯波斯享年75歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Pole
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Leicestershire): variant of   Paul   or   Poole  .
Americanized form of German   Pohle   or   Pohl  .

variants.name: Poles
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Livio, Primo, Santo.
English and Welsh: variant of Pole and Powell + genitival or excrescent -s.
Italian (Friuli and Veneto): probably a derivative of the personal name   Polo  . It is also found in Slovenia.

variants.name: Paul
meanings: English, French, German, Dutch, Swedish, West Indian (mainly Haiti, also e.g. Saint Lucia), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania): from the personal name Paul (from Latin Paulus ‘small’), which has always been popular in Christendom. It was the name adopted by the Pharisee Saul of Tarsus after his conversion to Christianity on the road to Damascus in about AD 34. He was a most energetic missionary to the Gentiles in the Roman Empire, and played a very significant role in establishing Christianity as a major world religion. The name was borne also by numerous other early Christian saints. It is also occasionally borne by Jews; the reasons for this are not clear. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages and their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Greek Pavlis , Slovenian Pavel and Pavlič (see Pavlic ), Polish Paweł (see Pawel ) and Pawlicki , Assyrian/Chaldean Polous and Polus . In France, this surname is most common in Brittany (see 2 below).
Breton (mainly Finistère): from a Frenchified form of the personal name Paol, Breton form of Paul .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phóil ‘son of Paul’. Compare McFall .

nameMeanings.name: Prince
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and French: nickname from Middle English, Old French prince (from Latin princeps).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Prinz ‘prince’, or its Czech, Slovak, Slovenian or some other Slavic cognate Princ, from princ ‘prince’.
Americanized form of Slovenian Primc: status name for one who lives in the house of his father-in-law, from a derivative of primiti ‘to accept, to take over’.

variants.name: Prinz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Matthias, Ulrich, Arno, Friedrich, Hans, Klaus, Mechthild.

German: nickname from Middle High German prinze ‘prince’, presumably denoting someone who behaved in a regal manner or who had won the title in some contest of skill. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Czechia.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): adoption of German Prinz ‘prince’.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Upper
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ernst, Manfred.  Scandinavian Hilmer.
German:
nickname from Middle High German dupper ‘idiot’.

variants.name: Tupper
meanings: English: variant of   Topper  .
German (Tüpper; North Rhine-Westphalia): occupational name for a potter, from Middle Low German duppe, Rhenish düppen ‘pot’. This surname is very rare in Germany.

History: This is the name of a family descended from two brothers, originally from Kassel, Germany. They fled religious persecution in the 16th century, settling in the Netherlands, where a descendant became burgomaster of Rotterdam in 1813. A branch of the family settled in England at Sandwich, Kent, whence another descendant, Thomas Tupper, went to America in 1635, and helped to found Sandwich, MA, in 1637. Benjamin Tupper, born in Stoughton, MA, in 1738 was a colonial legislator and explorer of OH.

nameMeanings.name: Powys
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Welsh:  see  Powis  .

variants.name: Powis
meanings: Welsh (now mainly West Midlands of England): habitational name from the region of Powis in mid Wales (from Old Welsh powes ‘country dwellers’).

nameMeanings.name: Gruffydd
meanings: Welsh: The Welsh name; from creffydd, strong faith, or strong in faith. Gruffydd, a Welsh form of Gryffydd.

en.lifeSummary: When Prince Gruffydd de la Pole - Prince of Upper Powys was born in 1211, in Montgomeryshire, Wales, United Kingdom, his father, Gwenwynwyn Cyfeiliog ap Gwenwynwyn, was 29 and his mother, Mary Corbet, was 24. He had at least 1 son with Mrs. Gruffydd la Pole. He died in 1286, in Powys, Wales, United Kingdom, at the age of 75.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Prince Gruffydd de la Pole - Prince of Upper Powys est né en 1211, à Montgomeryshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Gwenwynwyn Cyfeiliog ap Gwenwynwyn, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Mary Corbet, avait 24 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Mrs. Gruffydd la Pole. Il est décédé en 1286, à Powys, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 75 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Prince Gruffydd de la Pole - Prince of Upper Powys 1211 in Montgomeryshire, Wales, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gwenwynwyn Cyfeiliog ap Gwenwynwyn 29 und seine Mutter Mary Corbet 24. Er hatte mit Mrs. Gruffydd la Pole mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1286 im Alter von 75 Jahren in Powys, Wales, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Prince Gruffydd de la Pole - Prince of Upper Powys è nato il nel 1211 nel Montgomeryshire, Galles, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Gwenwynwyn Cyfeiliog ap Gwenwynwyn, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Mary Corbet, aveva 24 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Mrs. Gruffydd la Pole. Morì nel 1286, nel Powys, Galles, Regno Unito, all'età di 75 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Prince Gruffydd de la Pole - Prince of Upper Powys(이)가 1211년 영국 웨일스 몽고머이실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gwenwynwyn Cyfeiliog ap Gwenwynwyn(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Corbet(은)는 24세였다. 그는 Mrs. Gruffydd la Pole(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1286년 영국 웨일스 파우이스에서 75세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Prince Gruffydd de la Pole - Prince of Upper Powys nasceu em 1211, em Montgomeryshire, Gales, United Kingdom, seu pai, Gwenwynwyn Cyfeiliog ap Gwenwynwyn, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Mary Corbet, tinha 24 anos. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Prince Gruffydd de la Pole - Prince of Upper Powys родился в 1211 г. в местности монтгомиришир, Уэльс, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Gwenwynwyn Cyfeiliog ap Gwenwynwyn было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Corbet, 24. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Mrs. Gruffydd la Pole. Он умер в 1286 г. в местности поуис, Уэльс, Великобритания в возрасте 75 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Prince Gruffydd de la Pole - Prince of Upper Powys nació en 1211, en Montgomeryshire, Gales, Reino Unido, su papá, Gwenwynwyn Cyfeiliog ap Gwenwynwyn, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Mary Corbet, tenía 24 años.
